name: Fraction
javadoc:
/**
 * Representation of a rational number.
 *
 * implements Serializable since 2.0
 *
 * @since 1.1
 */

members.name: Fraction
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Create a fraction given the double value.

 * @throws FractionConversionException if the continued fraction failed to
 *         converge.
 */

tags.name: value
tags.content: the double value to convert to a fraction.
tags.description: the value must be a double

tags.content:
if the continued fraction failed to
        converge.

members.name: Fraction
members.javadoc:
/** * Create a fraction given the double value and maximum error allowed. *

* References: *

    *
  • * Continued Fraction equations (11) and (22)-(26)
    • *
*

* @param value the double value to convert to a fraction. * @param epsilon maximum error allowed. The resulting fraction is within * {@code epsilon} of {@code value}, in absolute terms. * @param maxIterations maximum number of convergents * @throws FractionConversionException if the continued fraction failed to * converge. */

tags.name: value
tags.content: the double value to convert to a fraction.
tags.description: the double value to convert to a fraction must be finite

tags.name: epsilon
tags.content:
maximum error allowed.  The resulting fraction is within
       {@code epsilon} of {@code value}, in absolute terms.
tags.description: the maximum error allowed must be non-negative

tags.name: maxIterations
tags.content: maximum number of convergents
tags.description: the maximum number of convergents must be positive

tags.name: FractionConversionException
tags.content:
if the continued fraction failed to
        converge.
tags.description: value or epsilon is negative

members.code:
/** * Create a fraction given the double value and maximum error allowed. *

* References: *

    *
  • * Continued Fraction equations (11) and (22)-(26)
    • *
*

* @param value the double value to convert to a fraction. * @param epsilon maximum error allowed. The resulting fraction is within * {@code epsilon} of {@code value}, in absolute terms. * @param maxIterations maximum number of convergents * @throws FractionConversionException if the continued fraction failed to * converge. */ public Fraction(double value, double epsilon, int maxIterations) throws FractionConversionException { this(value, epsilon, Integer.MAX_VALUE, maxIterations); }

members.name: Fraction
members.javadoc:
/** * Create a fraction given the double value and maximum denominator. *

* References: *

    *
  • * Continued Fraction equations (11) and (22)-(26)
    • *
*

* @param value the double value to convert to a fraction. * @param maxDenominator The maximum allowed value for denominator * @throws FractionConversionException if the continued fraction failed to * converge */

tags.name: value
tags.content: the double value to convert to a fraction.
tags.description: the double value must be finite

tags.name: maxDenominator
tags.content: The maximum allowed value for denominator
tags.description: The maximum allowed value for denominator must be positive

tags.name: FractionConversionException
tags.content:
if the continued fraction failed to
        converge
tags.description: the continued fraction failed to converge

members.code:
/** * Create a fraction given the double value and maximum denominator. *

* References: *

    *
  • * Continued Fraction equations (11) and (22)-(26)
    • *
*

* @param value the double value to convert to a fraction. * @param maxDenominator The maximum allowed value for denominator * @throws FractionConversionException if the continued fraction failed to * converge */ public Fraction(double value, int maxDenominator) throws FractionConversionException { this(value, 0, maxDenominator, 100); }

members.name: Fraction
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Create a fraction given the numerator and denominator.  The fraction is
 * reduced to lowest terms.
 * @param num the numerator.
 * @param den the denominator.
 * @throws MathArithmeticException if the denominator is {@code zero}
 */

tags.name: num
tags.content: the numerator.
tags.description: the numerator must be positive

tags.name: den
tags.content: the denominator.
tags.description: the denominator must be positive

tags.content: if the denominator is {@code zero}
tags.description: if the denominator is zero

members.name: equals
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Test for the equality of two fractions.  If the lowest term
 * numerator and denominators are the same for both fractions, the two
 * fractions are considered to be equal.
 * @param other fraction to test for equality to this fraction
 * @return true if two fractions are equal, false if object is
 *         {@code null}, not an instance of {@link Fraction}, or not equal
 *         to this fraction instance.
 */

tags.name: other
tags.content: fraction to test for equality to this fraction
tags.description: fraction to test for equality to this fraction

tags.content:
true if two fractions are equal, false if object is
        {@code null}, not an instance of {@link Fraction}, or not equal
        to this fraction instance.
tags.description:
true if two fractions are equal, false if object is
        {@code null}, not an instance of {@link Fraction}, or not equal
        to this fraction instance.

members.name: percentageValue
members.javadoc:
/** *

* Gets the fraction percentage as a {@code double}. This calculates the * fraction as the numerator divided by denominator multiplied by 100. *

* * @return the fraction percentage as a {@code double}. */

tags.content: the fraction percentage as a {@code double}.
tags.description: the fraction percentage as a double.

members.name: getReducedFraction
members.javadoc:
/** *

Creates a {@code Fraction} instance with the 2 parts * of a fraction Y/Z.

* *

Any negative signs are resolved to be on the numerator.

* * @param numerator the numerator, for example the three in 'three sevenths' * @param denominator the denominator, for example the seven in 'three sevenths' * @return a new fraction instance, with the numerator and denominator reduced * @throws MathArithmeticException if the denominator is {@code zero} */

tags.name: numerator
tags.content: the numerator, for example the three in 'three sevenths'
tags.description: the numerator must be an integer

tags.name: denominator
tags.content: the denominator, for example the seven in 'three sevenths'
tags.description: the denominator must be a positive integer

tags.content: a new fraction instance, with the numerator and denominator reduced
tags.description: a new fraction instance, with the numerator and denominator reduced

tags.name: MathArithmeticException
tags.content: if the denominator is {@code zero}
tags.description: if denominator is zero.

members.name: toString
members.javadoc:
/** *

* Returns the {@code String} representing this fraction, ie * "num / dem" or just "num" if the denominator is one. *

* * @return a string representation of the fraction. * @see java.lang.Object#toString() */

tags.content: a string representation of the fraction.
tags.description: a string representation of the fraction.

tags.content: java.lang.Object#toString()
